checks.finding: T-001 through T-005 are appropriately high risk; T-006 is appropriately medium risk. No issues found.
T-001 through T-005 require Red evidence before implementation, but do not require the failing test commit to precede its implementation commit, so mandatory TDD ordering is not provable.
T-001 through T-006 are coherent focused units and remain within the task-size bounds.
Each functional task names an associated error path, invalid-state case, or version-skew failure.
SKIP: no standalone task declares itself as a unit, integration, end-to-end, or acceptance test task.
Every high-risk task T-001 through T-005 has an explicit rollback field and exercised rollback evidence.
Shared files are sequenced by blockers; no uncoordinated parallel overlap was found.
The blockers reflect real artifact dependencies; no spurious or missing dependency was found.
T-001 records the symptom, diagnostic path, and regression command.
